rulesSupportedByScreenshots: Apple Fitness presents expanded Work/Recovery rows for repeat blocks.
Apple Fitness labels post-fixed-step residual movement as Open when fixed planned steps are exhausted.
Apple Fitness overview distinguishes workout time from elapsed time for paused workouts.
Paused row duration must be checked against active timer time and cannot be collapsed to wall-clock elapsed time.

rows.screenshotFinding: Apple Fitness expands the 800 m repeat block into individual Work and Recovery rows. Overview exposes workout time and elapsed time.
Apple Fitness expands the 800 m repeat block into individual Work and Recovery rows. Overview exposes workout time and elapsed time.
Apple Fitness labels post-fixed-cooldown residual movement as Open.
Apple Fitness expands mixed-distance repeat blocks into individual Work and Recovery rows. Overview exposes workout time and elapsed time.
Apple Fitness expands the 400 m repeat block into eight Work rows and eight Recovery rows, then Cooldown.
Apple Fitness expands the 1 km repeat block into individual Work and Recovery rows.

Apple Fitness expands the 400 m repeat block into ten Work rows and ten Recovery rows, then Cooldown. The summary shows a 61 second workout-time versus elapsed-time gap, matching the paired pause overlap in the RunSignal parity packet.
Apple Fitness overview exposes a 159 s workout-time versus elapsed-time split. The visible row duration aligns with active timer time for the paused cooldown.
Apple Fitness expands the 1 km repeat block into individual Work and Recovery rows.
Apple Fitness labels post-fixed-cooldown residual movement as Open.
Apple Fitness expands repeat rows and labels post-fixed-cooldown residual movement as Open.

noProductionChangeStatement: This scorecard is docs/debug validation only. It does not change Swift, production interval behavior, normal workout UI, HKWorkoutActivity promotion, FIT import/runtime usage, HealthFit dependency status, or Phase 3 implementation.
